pages.full_text:
__Saturday 15th October__
      We breakfasted at 8.. Jamie cooked us bacon. Jamie took us round the farm. He took us first to Simon's surprise - a vineyard! White grapes. Eventually 3 tons of juice a year!
      We also saw Mike's workshop - lovely cabins + Josh's dome + Toby + Ali's chestnut plantings barn. Crunchie was leading out the 2 horses to drag wood.
      After a cup of coffee we drove south to Wilsham House B + B, where I am hoping to celebrate my 60th birthday in July 2013. Richard + Jane welcomed us, showed us the farm (which has [?4] double bedrooms) explained about the B + B across the drive (which we couldn't see because it has people in) answered our questions + made suggestions. It's absolutely beautiful.
      Then we drove on past Lorna Doone's Farm to a nearby camp site, near __Oare__ ★ ★ ★
      Lots of girls doing D of E?
      On [the] to Barnstaple. Simon slept from 9 pm - 8 am, his ear + throat hurt + he's very tired, but better after a good sleep. It
pages.notes: Saturday 15th October with a mention of planning to celebrate a 60th birthday 'in July 2013' suggests this entry was written in October 2011 (when 15 Oct was a Saturday) or possibly October 2012 (when 15 Oct was also a Monday — so 2011 is more likely). The word after 'On' before 'to Barnstaple' appears to be 'the' crossed out or 'the way'. 'Crunchie' appears to be the name of a horse or animal. D of E = Duke of Edinburgh award. The number of double bedrooms is partially legible — could be 4 or another digit.

pages.full_text:
was warmer in the night.
      __Sunday__ On to Barnstaple where we managed to buy Micron + cycled along the Tarka Trail (Barnstaple → Instow Rd?)
[postcard: Appledore from Instow Quay, North Devon]
View from shop where we bought the cart
photo_captions: Appledore from Instow Quay, North Devon
View from shop where we bought the cart
pages.notes: The right page contains a glued-in postcard photograph titled 'Appledore from Instow Quay, North Devon'. A handwritten caption on the left side of the postcard reads 'View from shop where we bought the cart'. The arrow notation 'Barnstaple → Instow Rd?' may represent the direction cycled on the Tarka Trail. 'Micron' is unclear — could be a brand name for bikes or cycling equipment, or possibly 'Microns' (a hire shop). The handwritten page number '3' appears in the top-right corner. The Sunday date follows from Saturday 15th October, so ISO guess is the following day.
